div.h1: Wait Queue Lifecycle: Block → Sleep → Wakeup → Recheck → Proceed

div.p: Reader is OFF CPU. Waiting for one of two wakeup events: Event A: Writer calls wake_up_interruptible(&read_queue) Event B: Signal arrives (SIGINT, SIGKILL, etc.)
current process added to read_queue wait list
Task state: RUNNING → INTERRUPTIBLE Signals CAN interrupt this sleep
Task is OFF the CPU Scheduler runs other processes Reader is in TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE state on the wait queue
buffer_data_len += to_copy wake_up_interruptible(&read_queue) → Sets reader state: INTERRUPTIBLE → RUNNING → Adds reader to CPU run queue
SIGINT / SIGKILL / SIGTERM signal_pending(current) → true State: INTERRUPTIBLE → RUNNING macro returns -ERESTARTSYS
Another reader consumed data first, OR kernel issued a spurious wakeup. set_current_state(TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE) schedule() — sleep again on queue
